question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a TopicMessageFilter with a limit value close to Long.MAX_VALUE so that the cast `(int) (filter.getLimit() - context.getTotal().get())` overflows to a negative integer, causing `Math.min(limit, context.getMaxPageSize())` to return a negative pageSize and silently skip message delivery, leading to a subscriber missing critical fund-transfer confirmations?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a filter where `filter.getLimit()` is set to Integer.MAX_VALUE + 1 so that the narrowing cast `(int)(filter.getLimit() - context.getTotal().get())` wraps to a large negative number, causing `Flux.fromStream(topicMessageRepository.findByFilter(newFilter))` to be called with a negative limit and return zero messages, causing the subscriber to believe no funds were transferred when they were?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: retrieve()] Can an unprivileged external user open an unbounded throttled subscription (throttled=true) which sets `numRepeats = Long.MAX_VALUE`, holding a scheduler thread indefinitely and exhausting `Schedulers.boundedElastic()` thread pool, preventing other subscribers from receiving fund-transfer topic messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: retrieve()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger `Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, Duration.ofSeconds(1))` by causing repeated repository errors, creating an infinite retry loop that monopolizes scheduler resources and starves legitimate subscribers waiting for payment confirmation messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: isComplete()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the exact equality check `filter.getLimit() == total.get()` in `isComplete()` by causing `total` to skip past the limit value through a race condition in `onNext()`, so polling never terminates and the subscriber receives duplicate messages beyond the requested limit, causing double-processing of fund transfers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user set `startTime` to a value such that `last.getConsensusTimestamp() + 1` overflows a long, causing `startTime` to wrap to a very early timestamp and re-deliver all historical topic messages, leading to duplicate processing of fund-transfer events?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user provide a filter with `startTime` set to Long.MAX_VALUE - 1 so that `last.getConsensusTimestamp() + 1` overflows to Long.MIN_VALUE on the next poll, causing the query to fetch messages from the very beginning of the ledger and re-deliver all historical fund-transfer messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: PollingContext()] Can an unprivileged external user open many concurrent unthrottled subscriptions (throttled=false) each with `numRepeats = unthrottled.getMaxPolls()` (default 12) and `pollingFrequency = 20ms`, generating a burst of 12 rapid database queries per subscription, causing database overload that delays or drops fund-transfer message delivery for all other subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: retrieve()] Can an unprivileged external user open thousands of concurrent subscriptions to the same topicId, each creating a separate `PollingContext` and scheduler task, exhausting `Schedulers.boundedElastic()` and causing a denial-of-service that prevents fund-transfer confirmations from being delivered to legitimate subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user set `filter.getLimit()` to exactly 1 and exploit the race condition between `context.getTotal().get()` in `poll()` and `total.incrementAndGet()` in `onNext()` to receive 0 messages when 1 was expected, causing a missed fund-transfer confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: isComplete()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the throttled `isComplete()` condition `pageSize.get() < retrieverProperties.getMaxPageSize()` where `pageSize` is reset to 0 at the start of each `poll()` call, causing premature termination of the subscription before all fund-transfer messages are delivered when a page returns exactly maxPageSize messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a filter where `filter.hasLimit()` returns false (limit=0) so that `limit` is set to `Integer.MAX_VALUE`, and `pageSize = Math.min(Integer.MAX_VALUE, maxPageSize)` causes the repository to be queried with an extremely large page, triggering an OOM condition that crashes the gRPC service and prevents fund-transfer message delivery?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: retrieve()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `.timeout(retrieverProperties.getTimeout(), scheduler)` being applied after `.retryWhen(Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, ...))` to keep a subscription alive indefinitely by triggering retries that reset the timeout window, monopolizing scheduler resources and starving other fund-transfer subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: onNext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the non-atomic compound update across `last.set(topicMessage)`, `total.incrementAndGet()`, and `pageSize.incrementAndGet()` in `onNext()` to create a state inconsistency where `total` and `last` are out of sync, causing `poll()` to re-query from an incorrect `startTime` and skip or duplicate fund-transfer messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where `context.getLast()` returns a non-null `TopicMessage` but `last.getConsensusTimestamp()` returns 0, causing `startTime = 0 + 1 = 1` and fetching all messages from the beginning of the ledger, leading to massive re-delivery of historical fund-transfer messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: retrieve()] Can an unprivileged external user open a subscription with `throttled=false` and no limit, where `isComplete()` only returns true on `limitHit` (which is always false), causing the unthrottled poller to run for `maxPolls` iterations at 20ms intervals and then silently stop, causing the subscriber to miss fund-transfer messages that arrive after the polling window closes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: PollingContext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `throttled=false` with `numRepeats = unthrottled.getMaxPolls()` (default 12) causes the subscription to terminate after 12 polls regardless of whether all messages have been delivered, causing a subscriber to miss fund-transfer messages that arrive after the 12th poll?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `newFilter = filter.toBuilder().limit(pageSize).startTime(startTime).build()` construction to bypass `TopicMessageFilter` validation constraints (e.g., `isValidStartTime`) since the builder is called internally without re-validation, allowing a crafted startTime to fetch messages outside the intended range?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: retrieve()] Can an unprivileged external user open a throttled subscription with no endTime constraint, causing the poller to run indefinitely (`numRepeats = Long.MAX_VALUE`) and continuously query the database, creating a sustained load that degrades database performance and delays fund-transfer message delivery for all subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: isComplete()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the throttled `isComplete()` check `pageSize.get() < retrieverProperties.getMaxPageSize()` where `pageSize` is reset to 0 at the beginning of each `poll()` call before messages are counted, creating a TOCTOU window where `isComplete()` is evaluated with stale `pageSize=0` and incorrectly terminates the subscription, dropping fund-transfer messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user cause `Flux.fromStream(topicMessageRepository.findByFilter(newFilter))` to reuse a closed Java Stream by triggering a retry after the stream has been consumed, causing an `IllegalStateException` that terminates the subscription and drops pending fund-transfer messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: retrieve()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ordering of operators—`.retryWhen()` before `.timeout()`—so that a timeout event is not retried but a repository error is retried indefinitely, causing a subscription to silently stall without delivering fund-transfer messages while consuming scheduler resources?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: onComplete()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger `onComplete()` prematurely by cancelling a subscription mid-stream, causing the log message to report an incorrect `total` count that misleads operators into believing all fund-transfer messages were delivered when they were not?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user set `filter.getLimit()` to a value where `filter.getLimit() - context.getTotal().get()` produces a value larger than Integer.MAX_VALUE, causing the `(int)` cast to silently truncate the limit and cause `findByFilter` to return fewer messages than expected, resulting in missed fund-transfer messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: retrieve()] Can an unprivileged external user open many subscriptions with `throttled=true` and a very high `startTime` (near current time), causing each subscription to poll at `pollingFrequency` (default 2s) indefinitely, creating a sustained amplification attack where N subscribers generate N/2 database queries per second?
